questions: Is the deployed CLI actually running the hardened retry/timeout code?
Why does the CLI complete in ~310ms for 504 errors when retry backoff should take 6000ms+?
What changes are needed in the CLI repo to fix the runtime logs problem?

answers.answer: No. The installed npm package @projectxinnovation/helix-cli@1.2.0 contains the pre-hardening code: single fetch(), no retry loop, no AbortSignal.timeout, process.exit(1) on any non-OK response. The source repo hx-cli has the hardened version at the same version number (1.2.0) but it was never published.
evidence: Deployed dist/lib/http.js: bare 'const response = await fetch(url.toString(), { method, headers, body })', no retry loop, no timeout.
Source src/lib/http.ts: MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, BASE_DELAY_MS=2000, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000, RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES set with 429, 500, 502, 503, 504.
Both package.json files show version 1.2.0 — version was bumped in source but never republished.

answers.answer: The CLI completes in ~310ms (250ms Node startup + 80ms single HTTP request) because the deployed code makes exactly one fetch call and immediately calls process.exit(1) on failure. There is no retry loop in the deployed binary.
evidence: hlx inspect db for SELECT 1/0: total 310ms, exit code 1.
Direct curl for same query: 80ms for 504 response.
Node startup overhead: ~250ms (difference between hlx total time and curl time).
Deployed http.js: no retry loop, no sleep/backoff code, just process.exit(1).

answers.answer: The CLI source code changes are already complete — the retry logic, timeouts, and error classification exist in src/lib/http.ts. The only required action is to rebuild and republish the npm package. However, this alone is insufficient because the server-side error handling bug (RC1) means all query errors still produce opaque 502/504 HTML. The server fix must come first.
evidence: Source http.ts has: MAX_ATTEMPTS=3, BASE_DELAY_MS=2000, REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=30000, RETRYABLE_STATUS_CODES=[429,500,502,503,504], HTML error page detection, backoff with jitter.
Deployed http.js has: none of the above.
Server RC1 means even with CLI retries, all 3 attempts would get 504 for any error query.

statement: The CLI hardening (retry logic, timeouts, error classification) exists in source but was never published to npm. The deployed @projectxinnovation/helix-cli@1.2.0 has the old single-fetch, process.exit(1) code. The source changes are already correct and just need to be published. However, the server must first be fixed (RC1: add try/catch in inspection controllers) — without proper server error responses, CLI retries would just retry the same 504 three times.
